aggregate_classification.rationale: Both windows classify as simulator_pessimistic. Aggregate certainty limited by: (1) only 2 windows instead of 3+ target, (2) 1 of 2 windows (#3028) venue/regime-confounded, (3) no regime scorecards available, (4) proxy-only calibration without explicit reject data. Aggregate would not change with additional same-venue windows unless window 3+ shows different drift direction.

limitations: proxy-only calibration, no explicit reject data
regime_discrepancy: regime_id=0 (TREND default) vs original regime_id=2 (HIGH_VOL_CHAOTIC)
confounded drift: venue/regime differences may explain deltas, not pure simulator behavior
proxy-only calibration, no explicit reject data
signal delta of -1 likely venue/regime-related (pilot had signal_delta=0)

ranked_findings.finding: Simulator consistently misses orders/fills in same-venue conditions
ranked_findings.detail: If the simulator systematically misses orders that paper would execute, canary performance projections from replay-only evidence would be overconfident

ranked_findings.finding: Signal generation differs with venue/regime change
ranked_findings.detail: Signal delta of -1 may be venue or regime related, not simulator-only. Cannot quantify without same-venue MEXC data
Consistent fill gap (-1/-1) across both windows suggests systematic simulator limit, but n=2 is too small for confident pattern claim

ranked_findings.finding: No explicit reject data available in any window
ranked_findings.detail: Proxy calibration is documented limitation from #1903 contract; explicit reject handling is a known gap

execution_realism_candidate_gap.gap: Fill model / order execution realism in replay
execution_realism_candidate_gap.evidence: Both windows show fill_count_delta=-1. Replay produces 0 fills where paper produced 1 fill. Consistent pattern across same-venue and venue-mismatched windows.
execution_realism_candidate_gap.note: Signal delta differs between windows (0 vs -1). The order/fill delta is the cleaner signal for simulator realism assessment.

venue_regime_caveats: Regime discrepancy: #3028 uses default regime_id=0 (TREND), original paper used regime_id=2 (HIGH_VOL_CHAOTIC)
Price differences between Binance and MEXC affect breakout detection
#3028 drift classification confounded by venue/regime differences, not pure simulator behavior
safety_boundaries: No runtime execution beyond committed artifacts
Binance candles are not MEXC evidence
Drift classification is diagnosis, not trade authorization
No batch conclusion without explicit limitations
#3028 drift classification confounded by venue/regime differences
